coverage_profile.seniority_signal: Managing Director title appears throughout the record and he regularly opens the Q&A (was the first questioner on 11 of 16 calls), and management addresses him by name and with familiarity. This behavior and his access to multi-topic, high-level questions signal senior, repeat coverage of RCL.
coverage_profile.sector_lens: Cruise / leisure / travel focus (Gaming & Leisure coverage flagged in his Stifel roster). He frames questions around demand, yields, onboard spend, capacity deployment and capital allocation rather than micro hotel/airline minutiae.
rhetorical_signature: opens with "Hey, guys. Good morning." in 12 of 16 calls
frequent two-part structure: prefacing with praise or housekeeping then a financial question (e.g. "Nice quarter" then Trifecta/EBITDA follow-up)
closes politely and succinctly: "Thanks, guys" / "Appreciate it" are common sign-offs

reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern: Tends to ask multi-part questions (two-part or multi-part) and pushes for clarifications; ≈55% of his turns contain ≥2 sub-questions or explicit follow-ups. He will both drill into modeling (numbers) and probe strategic implications.
reasoning_style.evidence_demanded: He repeatedly asks for explicit numeric anchors and guidance (examples: "$5 billion + of 2023 debt maturities" — 2022-Q2; "a number like 4x" leverage — 2022-Q3; "1% change in our yields is $120 million" — 2024-Q1). n_turns requesting explicit numeric targets or anchors (examples above) = 9 distinct turns (2022-Q2, 2022-Q3, 2022-Q3 housekeeping, 2023-Q1, 2023-Q2, 2023-Q4, 2024-Q1, 2024-Q3, 2025-Q2). He does not use 'bps' in his questions (n_turns using "bps" = 0).
reasoning_style.anchoring_habits: He anchors frequently to prior guidance and management-provided coordinates (e.g., pre-2019 baselines and Trifecta/Profecta coordinates). Examples: referenced pre-COVID leverage targets like "three and a half times" (2022-Q3), referenced competitor equity moves earlier in the cycle (2022-Q2), and routinely picks up the company's recently-stated handles such as the "$14 handle" (2024-Q3) or "$17 handle" (2025-Q3). He also occasionally anchors to peers' actions (cites a competitor issuing equity in 2022-Q2).

blind_spots: No evidence in this history of questions on fuel hedging strategy (0 turns explicitly on fuel hedging) or loyalty-program KPIs (guest segmentation / member penetration) despite their materiality to yield and repeat business. Several peers and management discussed fuel/FX headwinds, but Steven did not press on hedging mechanics in any documented turn.
stance_drift: Early in the window (2022-Q1/2022-Q2) his emphasis was on liquidity and refinancing (cash flow sustainability and 2023 maturities). By 2023-Q1/2023-Q2 the focus shifted to hitting Trifecta/earnings coordinates and like-for-like yield decomposition. By 2024-Q3/2024-Q4 he increasingly pressed on capital allocation and private-destination ROI (Perfect Day Mexico) and buyback inclusion in EPS handles.

queue_behavior.typical_position: #1 on 11 of 16 calls; otherwise he appears early (typically #2–#3) when not first.
queue_behavior.default_first_pick: When first/early he usually opens on company-level financials: bookings/yield/earnings trajectory or balance-sheet questions (examples as opener: 2022-Q1 on cash-flow inflection; 2022-Q4 on book position; 2023-Q1 on EBITDA trajectory; 2024-Q1 on 2025 bookings).

reframe_deeper.evidence: Yes — when topics are already raised he drills deeper (asks decomposition or modeling follow-ups). Example: asked to "break down those yields between ticket and onboard" after yield commentary (quote: "breaking yields down between ticket and onboard" — 2022-Q3).

reactive_followup.evidence: Yes — he frequently picks up management's freshly-delivered numbers/phrases and asks a follow-up that reconciles to them (e.g., asked whether record EBITDA referenced the 3.6 billion base after management said 3.6% — quote: "is that going off of an EBITDA base in 2019 of $3.6 billion or 3.4 billion?" — 2022-Q3).

stay_on_topic_anyway.evidence: Yes — even when peers have already asked a topic he will re-ask or press the same topic (bookings, yields, Trifecta). Example: multiple calls where he returned to Trifecta/earnings timing after others had discussed it (2023-Q2, 2023-Q4).

white_space_topics.topic: private-destination capex/port-acquisition mechanics (Perfect Day Mexico specifics)

explicit_peer_references.examples: no evidence in history of him explicitly saying "following up on X's question" or naming another analyst before his question.

examples: quoted the 3.6% / $3.6 billion EBITDA base question (asked "is that going off of an EBITDA base in 2019 of $3.6 billion or 3.4 billion?") — 2022-Q3
picked up management's "$14 handle" phrase and asked whether buybacks were included — 2024-Q3

cross_analyst_reactivity.operator_or_followup_signal: He frequently takes a second turn within the same call: observed taking follow-up/second turns on about 9 of the 16 calls and primarily uses them for clarification or to drill deeper on modeling and assumptions (examples: 2022-Q1 follow-up on liquidity; 2022-Q2 follow-up on timing of costs; 2024-Q3 follow-up on $14 handle / buybacks).

framing_overrides_by_theme.booking_dynamics: Anchors on booked position vs optimal and tests willingness to flex load factor versus price in a slowdown.
framing_overrides_by_theme.capital_allocation_pattern: Anchors on the path back to investment grade and tests whether EPS guides embed or exclude buybacks/refis.
framing_overrides_by_theme.fuel_hedging_dynamics: Reconciles EPS guide against quantified FX and fuel headwinds and asks what offsets (buybacks, refis) close the gap.
framing_overrides_by_theme.yield_decomposition: Breaks yields into ticket vs onboard and probes whether 'moderate yield growth' tagline still fits; pushes on yield-cost spread in bps and PDMx uplift for 2027.
